data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the issues in `ItemManagement.csproj`:

1. **Duplicate source file warnings**: The `<Compile Include="**\*.cs">` glob on line 9 already picks up all `.cs` files (including `Constants.g.cs`). The SDK also auto-includes `**/*.cs` by default. So there are duplicates from: (a) SDK default glob, (b) the explicit `**\*.cs` glob, and (c) the `Constants.g.cs` include in the GenerateConstants target.

2. **Validation target cross-join**: The `ValidateData` target uses `%(DataFile.Filename)` and `%(SchemaFile.Filename)` in the same Message task. Since these are two independent item groups, MSBuild will produce a cross-product (cartesian join) — 2×2 = 4 messages instead of the intended 2 (users→users, orders→orders). Need to link them via metadata.


3. **Generated files go to source tree**: The `GenerateConstants` target writes directly to the project directory instead of the intermediate output path where build artifacts belong.
4. **dotnet clean doesn't remove generated files**: I need to register the generated file in the `FileWrites` item and add a clean target to properly remove it during cleanup.

Let me fix all these issues.

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Mark generated C# files for IDE purposes without duplicating the SDK default glob -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Update="**\*.g.cs">
      <AutoGen>true</AutoGen>
    </Compile>
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- Data files paired with their validation schemas via metadata -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <DataFile Include="data\users.json">
      <Schema>schemas\users.xsd</Schema>
    </DataFile>
    <DataFile Include="data\orders.json">
      <Schema>schemas\orders.xsd</Schema>
    </DataFile>
  </ItemGroup>

  <Target Name="ValidateData" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <!-- Validate each data file against its paired schema (one iteration per DataFile) -->
    <Message Text="Validating %(DataFile.Filename) against %(DataFile.Schema)"
             Importance="high" />
  </Target>

  <!-- Generate constants file into the intermediate output directory -->
  <Target Name="GenerateConstants" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)Constants.g.cs"
                     Lines="namespace ItemManagement { public static class Constants { public const int Version = 1; } }"
                     Overwrite="true" />
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)Constants.g.cs" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  <!-- Track generated files so dotnet clean removes them -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <FileWrites Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)Constants.g.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Here's what I fixed:

1. **Duplicate warnings** — Changed `<Compile Include="**\*.cs">` to `<Compile Update="**\*.g.cs">`. The SDK already auto-includes all `.cs` files; the extra `Include` created duplicates. `Update` modifies existing items without adding new ones.

2. **Validation cross-join** — Removed the separate `SchemaFile` item group and instead attached the schema as metadata (`<Schema>`) on each `DataFile`. The message now batches on `%(DataFile.Filename)` and `%(DataFile.Schema)`, running exactly once per data file.

3. **Generated file location** — Changed `$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\Constants.g.cs` to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)Constants.g.cs` so output goes to `obj/` instead of the source tree.

4. **Clean support** — Added a `<FileWrites>` item for the generated file, which tells `dotnet clean` to delete it.
